summary: Phase 4A iter256 — BACKEND fully PASSES (28/28 phase3+phase4a unit + 6/6 new live smoke against the public preview URL = 34 backend test pass). FRONTEND is fully BLOCKED by a Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ) ReferenceError thrown by UnifiedSalesPage.js on mount: 'Cannot access paymentType before initialization'. The whole /sales-new page crashes inside React's first render so no FE-1..FE-11 assertion can be exercised. Root cause is a hook-ordering mistake in the new Phase 4A code: isHistoricalCreditMode (lines 485-489) and isBackdatedNonCreditBlocked (lines 495-497) reference `paymentType` inside their useMemo bodies, but `const [paymentType, setPaymentType] = useState('cash')` is not declared until line 533 — same component scope, JS TDZ raises ReferenceError before the memos can run. Backend soft-floor (BACKEND-1, BACKEND-2), approval gate (BACKEND-3, BACKEND-4), list (BACKEND-5), and unified-sale today-cash regression (BACKEND-6) all behave exactly as specified.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught ReferenceError: Cannot access 'paymentType' before initialization. The `isHistoricalCreditMode` useMemo (line ~485) and `isBackdatedNonCreditBlocked` useMemo (line ~495) read `paymentType`, but `useState('cash')` for paymentType is declared at line ~533 (later in the same function body). JavaScript TDZ rejects the read and React unmounts the entire page tree to the global error overlay. This blocks ALL of FE-1..FE-11. Fix: move the `paymentType` (and any sibling state the memos touch — verify `daysBack` is fine since it is declared at line ~469 BEFORE the memos and works) useState declarations above the new Phase 4A memos, OR move the `isHistoricalCreditMode` / `isBackdatedNonCreditBlocked` memo definitions below the useState block (line ~533+).

integration_issues.flow: Historical Credit / Notebook AR full UI flow (banner → reason → preview → TOTP → commit)
integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised end-to-end because UnifiedSalesPage crashes before mount. Re-test required after the TDZ fix.

action_items: P0: Fix the TDZ ordering in /app/frontend/src/pages/UnifiedSalesPage.js. Either (a) move `const [paymentType, setPaymentType] = useState('cash')` (line ~533) and any sibling state read by the new memos to ABOVE the `isHistoricalCreditMode` (line ~485) and `isBackdatedNonCreditBlocked` (line ~495) useMemo blocks, or (b) relocate those two new useMemo blocks to AFTER the entire useState section. Make sure hook-call order remains stable across renders.

P0: After the TDZ fix lands, re-run the FE-1..FE-11 playbook (login as test_org_admin@regression.local → /sales-new → add cart item → flip date 14 days back → toggle credit/cash/digital/split → verify banner data-testids → run preview → enter wrong TOTP → assert 403 surfaced).

P1: Add a tiny smoke test (jest/RTL or just a Playwright load assertion) that mounts UnifiedSalesPage and asserts no `Uncaught ReferenceError` is logged — this whole class of bug should fail in CI, not at user-side.
critical_code_review_comments: Phase 4A frontend regression — the new memos at lines 485-497 of UnifiedSalesPage.js read `paymentType` from a useState declared later in the same component body. This is a TDZ ReferenceError on the very first render. Hook-ordering / declaration-ordering inside a 5,500-line component is fragile; consider extracting Phase 4A state and memos into a custom hook (e.g. useHistoricalCreditMode(transactionDate, paymentType, user)) so the dependency direction is explicit and lint-detectable.

/app/backend/routes/historical_credit.py soft-floor logic in _validate_payload now correctly raises 400 use_regular_late_encode for days_back 1..7 — verified via live API. Implementation order (errors first, then soft_floor_violation) is the right call since it surfaces grand_total/customer_id problems before the 7-day gate.

/app/backend/routes/historical_credit.py commit endpoint correctly returns 400 approval_code_required and 403 approval_invalid as specified — verified via live API against the regression admin (no TOTP enrolled) so the wrong-code path was exercised end-to-end.
Live smoke: GET /api/historical-credit returns 200 (admin-only list) and POST /api/unified-sale today's cash sale still returns 200 — no Phase 4A backend regression on the regular Sales path.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Phase 4A is GREEN. The remaining work is purely frontend — once the main agent fixes the paymentType TDZ ordering bug in UnifiedSalesPage.js, re-run the FE-1..FE-11 playbook from the iter256 review request. Re-run cleanups: (1) `python3 -c "...delete_many(username regex admin-[0-9a-f]{4})..."` cleared 1,602 leaked test users from previous fork runs — re-run if you see DuplicateKeyError on admin-* in the phase3/phase4a unit suites. (2) Live smoke command: `cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://br-suite-phase5.preview.emergentagent.com python3 -m pytest tests/test_phase4a_live_smoke.py -v`. (3) Phase 4A backend pytest: `cd /app/backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_phase3_historical_credit.py tests/test_phase4a_approval_gate.py -v` → expect 28 passed.
rca of the issue: REPRODUCTION: Login as test_org_admin@regression.local at /login, click 'Sales' in the sidebar (route resolves to /sales-new which renders UnifiedSalesPage). Page tree never mounts; React's dev overlay shows: 'ReferenceError: Cannot access paymentType before initialization at UnifiedSalesPage (bundle.js:484544:187)'. ROOT CAUSE: The two new Phase 4A useMemo blocks at lines 485-489 (isHistoricalCreditMode) and 495-497 (isBackdatedNonCreditBlocked) reference `paymentType` inside their bodies and dependency arrays, but `const [paymentType, setPaymentType] = useState('cash')` is hoisted-as-uninitialized at line 533 of the same component scope (TDZ). JavaScript correctly throws ReferenceError on first render, before any memo can compute. MITIGATION: Move the paymentType useState (and verify daysBack/isPrivilegedRole are still resolved earlier — they are at lines 469 and 426 respectively, so they are fine) above the new Phase 4A memos. Alternative: move the two new memos to after the full useState block (~line 533+). Either change preserves React hook-call order across renders. Once landed, FE-1..FE-11 should be re-executed end-to-end.
